growth_records.medium: Modified DCB-1 chemically defined mineral medium

up.method: UPLC-APCI-HRMS / HPLC quantification of substrate depletion; ~2.3 mM consumed over 15 d (=360 h); rate=2.3/360. Not normalisable to gDW (biomass reported only as cells/mL: 2.3e4 -> 1.3e7 cells/mL).

sec.method: UPLC-APCI-HRMS/TLC; 0.9 mM total estrogen produced over 15 d (360 h); rate=0.9/360
UPLC-APCI-HRMS/TLC; 1.4 mM produced over 15 d (360 h); rate=1.4/360

Not directly quantified; the removed steroidal C-19 methyl group is inferred (transcriptomics/proteomics + 13C-testosterone tracing) to be fully oxidised to CO2 via the oxidative Wood-Ljungdahl pathway; no acetate formed (acetate kinase absent).

growth_records.cite: Wang, Po-Hsiang et al. Bacterial estrogenesis without oxygen: Wood–Ljungdahl pathway likely contributed to the emergence of estrogens in the biosphere. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2422930122. [PMC11912376]
growth_records.snippet: After 15 d of anaerobic incubation, ~2.3 mM testosterone was consumed, yielding 0.9 mM estrogen and 1.4 mM androstandiol. During growth, strain TUW77 cell density increased from ~2.3 × 10^4 cells/mL to 1.3 × 10^7 cells/mL over 15 d, with a
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic testosterone-fermentation condition. Growth: doubling time 39.2 h reported directly; mu calculated mu=ln2/td=0.693/39.2=0.0177 /h (growth_method=calculated). Rates are VOLUMETRIC (mmol/L/h) because biomass is given only as cells/mL (2.3e4->1.3e7 over 15 d), so gDW normalisation is impossible. Testosterone qS=2.3 mM/(15 d x 24 h=360 h)=0.0064 mmol/L/h. Estrogen (estrone+estradiol) q=0.9/360=0.0025; androstanediol q=1.4/360=0.0039 mmol/L/h. Product yields: estrogen/testosterone=0.9/2.3=0.39 mol/mol; androstanediol/testosterone=1.4/2.3=0.61 mol/mol (sum~1.0, consistent with testosterone fully partitioned to the two products). CO2 production inferred (oxidative WLP), not quantified. DCB-1 mineral medium composition is cited (ref 17), not stated in text -> medium_composition_stated=false; only testosterone (1 mM) given. Temp 30C, N2/CO2 80:20 headspace, dark. pH not stated. 13C-testosterone tracing done but no net MFA flux table reported.

growth_records.medium: Modified DCB-1 chemically defined mineral medium + AQDS

up.method: UPLC-APCI-HRMS/TLC; complete testosterone conversion observed over 14 d, but no time-resolved concentration/rate reported for this condition

sec.method: TLC/UPLC-HRMS; with AQDS, testosterone fully converted to estrogen with NO androstanediol accumulation (contrast to fermentation); no quantitative rate reported
Inferred full oxidation of the C-19 methyl group to CO2 via oxidative WLP with electrons routed to AQDS; not quantified

product_yields.note: Under AQDS, testosterone->estrogen only (no androstanediol); complete conversion, but no numeric mM/rate reported in main text

growth_records.cite: Wang, Po-Hsiang et al. Bacterial estrogenesis without oxygen: Wood–Ljungdahl pathway likely contributed to the emergence of estrogens in the biosphere. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2422930122. [PMC11912376]
growth_records.snippet: the addition of anthraquinone-2,6-disulfonate, an extracellular electron acceptor, to testosterone-fed TUW77 cultures enabled complete testosterone conversion into estrogen without androstanediol accumulation (anaerobic testosterone oxidati
growth_records.notes: Distinct condition = anaerobic testosterone oxidation with AQDS as terminal electron acceptor (vs the fermentation record above). Qualitatively: complete testosterone -> estrogen conversion, no androstanediol, 14 d incubation. No time-resolved concentrations, no growth rate, no doubling time reported for this specific condition in the main text, so all rate/growth fields null (values likely in SI Fig. 4). Included as a separate experimental condition per one-record-per-condition rule. Medium DCB-1 composition cited (ref 17) not stated; AQDS concentration not stated in text -> medium_composition_stated=false.
